scenes.speech: The human brain — recreated in a computer! Let's see how it works!
scenes.title: Neural Networks

scenes.speech: Your brain has billions of neurons!
scenes.title: What is a Neuron?
scenes.content: The human brain has about 86 billion neurons. They communicate via electrical signals, creating thoughts, emotions, and memories!

scenes.title: Artificial Neurons
scenes.content: Numbers go in, calculations happen, results pass to the next unit. Connecting many simple units enables complex behavior!

scenes.speech: Neural networks are organized in layers!
scenes.title: Layers
scenes.content: Input layer → Hidden layers → Output layer. Deeper layers learn more complex patterns. This is why it's called 'Deep Learning'!

scenes.title: Weights and Biases
scenes.content: Each connection has a 'weight'. Training adjusts weights to get closer to the correct answer. Like tuning a musical instrument!

scenes.title: Activation Functions
scenes.content: Functions like ReLU and Sigmoid decide which neurons 'activate'. Without them, complex patterns can't be learned!

scenes.title: Backpropagation
scenes.content: When prediction is wrong, the error is sent backwards to adjust weights. This is the heart of machine learning!

scenes.title: CNNs for Images
scenes.content: CNNs capture local features in images. Used in smartphone face unlock and self-driving car object detection!

scenes.speech: Neural networks specialized for text and audio!
scenes.title: RNNs for Language
scenes.content: RNNs handle sequential data and are used for translation and text generation. The basis of ChatGPT!

scenes.speech: You understand neural networks now! Amazing!
scenes.title: Lesson Complete!
